lines: After a hard-fought battle, Granny was finally defeated!
The idea of this ruthless mogul being bested by a bartender who just learned of her
whipped and beat Granny’s ego beyond repair.
*GASP*
'You… fought well kid…'
*COUGH*
'Just hurry up and finish the job'
'No', Duke Proclaimed
'What?!', Granny struggled to make out the words through her coughing fit
'You… you’re… keeping me alive?'
'Did I hit your head that hard?'
'Well then, keep your silly little saloon!'
'We can *GAG* we can just become *GAG* business partners in the future...'
Her and all of her lackeys, being soundly bested, humbled and slightly inebriated,
all left the saloon.
And, after a lengthy, yawn-inducing diatribe from Teddy,
Granny finally agreed to relinquish control of the bars to their rightful owners.
And thus, Hero’s Haven was saved!
And Duke’s victory over Granny and her goons made the saloon even more popular.
Duke, Bobby, Abby, and even Teddy celebrated the night away.
'For Duke’s heroism,' Abby declared,'Hero’s Haven stands for another day!'
Though, Duke wasn’t fully content.
As daybreak came, Duke stood outside, alone with his thoughts.
Having spent his day defending his saloon and friends from Granny’s ferocious conglomerate
Duke finally was able to forgive himself for leaving Yonder.
Though, his love for those townspeople and his sense of justice has never faltered.
So, one day...
maybe tomorrow,
maybe next month,
maybe in a year,
Duke will return to the Town of Yonder, armed with a newfound sense of purpose,
To set things right.
But that’s a story for another day…
